channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The ongoing impact of the Christchurch attack and Australia’s role in confronting Islamophobia
item.description: The Christchurch mosque shootings in New Zealand on 15 March 2019, perpetrated by an Australian, continue to echo through legal cases and discussions in Australia. Young offenders in South Australia h...

item.title: Northern Ontario woman passes away unexpectedly during vacation in the Dominican Republic
item.description: A woman from Northern Ontario tragically died while on vacation in the Dominican Republic. The sudden nature of her passing has shocked her family and community back home. Local authorities in the Dom...

item.title: Lindokuhle Mathebula Keeps Sekhukhune United at Bay in Goalless Limpopo Derby
item.description: The Limpopo derby between Polokwane City and Sekhukhune United ended in a 0-0 stalemate, much to the frustration of Sekhukhune United coach Eric Tinkler. The first half was largely uneventful, but the...

item.title: Harambee Stars Squad Announced with Vyner and Obiero for March FIFA Friendlies
item.description: Harambee Stars head coach Benni McCarthy has named a 23-man squad for the upcoming FIFA Series friendlies in Kigali, Rwanda, from March 23 to 31, 2026. The team features a mix of experienced overseas ...

item.title: Telangana Police Report 100 Stray Dogs Killed by Poisoning
item.description: According to the available information, the Telangana police have reported that approximately 100 stray dogs were found dead due to poisoning in the state. The incident has raised concern among animal...

item.title: Comelec Announces Results of Antipolo Second District Special Election
item.description: Voters in Antipolo City participated in a special election on Saturday to choose a new congressman for the second district, following the passing of Rep. Romeo Acop last December. Comelec Chair George...

item.title: Teaching Students Critical Thinking Through Difficult Current Events in K-12 Education
item.description: K-12 classrooms across the United States often encounter spontaneous discussions about challenging topics, such as the Iran war, that teachers may feel unprepared to manage. Many educators, acting in ...

item.title: How Musa Juma shaped Kenyan rhumba and continues to inspire fans 15 years after his death
item.description: Musa Juma, popularly known as MJ Wuod Usonga, was a leading rhumba and benga musician from Alego Usonga in Siaya whose music blended traditional Luo benga with Congolese rhumba to create a polished, c...

item.title: Challenges and Legislative Efforts for Plug-In Solar Panels in the U.S.
item.description: Plug-in solar panels, sometimes called 'balcony' panels, offer a simple way for homeowners to generate electricity by plugging directly into an outlet, potentially offsetting energy costs for applianc...

item.title: Two Arrested in Connection to Burnaby Man’s Death
item.description: Two individuals have been formally charged with first-degree murder following the discovery of 45-year-old Masood Masjoody’s remains in Mission, B.C., earlier this month. The Integrated Homicide Inves...

item.title: Australia boosts fuel stockpiles to support regional supply amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Australia has bolstered its fuel reserves to ensure regional supply amid ongoing global tensions following the US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Energy Minister Chris Bowen confirmed the country now hold...

item.title: Pakistan Medical Association Warns of Critical Shortage in Child TB Drugs Leading to Risk of Drug Resistance
item.description: The Pakistan Medical Association (PMA) has sounded a serious alarm about the ongoing severe shortage of essential paediatric tuberculosis (TB) medicines across Pakistan. They say this shortage has rea...

item.description: Bugando University, officially known as the Catholic University of Health and Allied Sciences (CUHAS Bugando) in Mwanza, Tanzania, is now accepting applications for the 2026/2027 academic year. The un...

item.title: Arsenal extend lead in Premier League as West Ham draw with Manchester City
item.description: For this weekend Premier League gist, Arsenal dey ginger for top spot after dem beat Everton 2-0 for Emirates Stadium. The match no easy as Everton hold their ground, but Viktor Gyökeres knack the fir...

item.title: Industrial pollution in Gauteng linked to sulphur emissions affecting workers and communities
item.description: Residents across parts of Johannesburg have reported a strong 'rotten egg' smell in recent days, which the Association of Mineworkers and Construction Union (Amcu) says is linked to industrial air pol...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk released after Centre revokes National Security Act detention
item.description: New Delhi: Veteran climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from Jodhpur Jail on Saturday following the revocation of his detention under the National Security Act (NSA) by the Union government. W...

item.title: Oyo State Taskforce Impounds Nine Cattle to Protect Ijaye Farm Settlement
item.description: The Oyo State Rule of Law Enforcement Authority (OYRLEA) carried out an operation on Friday to impound nine cattle that were freely roaming and grazing inside Ijaye Farm Settlement, located in Akinyel...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay trains in the pool for upcoming Ironman event, inspired by son-in-law Adam Peaty
item.description: Celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay, aged 59, has been spotted training for an Ironman triathlon by focusing on swimming at a pool in the Toronto Athletic Club, Canada. He shared images on his Instagram Stor...

item.title: Halifax Council Approves Large-Scale Housing Development on Mic Mac Mall Parking Lots
item.description: Halifax has approved a major development that will transform the parking lots surrounding Mic Mac Mall in Dartmouth into a new neighbourhood designed for thousands of residents. The project, spearhead...

item.title: Campaigners urge NHS trusts to avoid adopting Palantir's Federated Data Platform over privacy and ethical concerns
item.description: A coalition of doctors, human rights organisations, and campaign groups, including Medact and Amnesty International, has called on NHS hospitals and trusts in England to reconsider or reject the use o...
